human_verification.notes: Reviewed healthcare IT systems boundaries, EHR/interface/IAM/change/downtime artifact expectations, PHI and patient-safety handling, clinical/security/privacy/payer/release escalation rules, source authority mapping, research summary, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical workflow changes, treatment decisions, payer interpretation, authorization approval, PHI disclosure, access/security exceptions, release approval, and unauthorized production changes.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers interface failures across orders/results/charges, RBAC/privacy access conflicts, untested production fixes, PHI-sensitive artifacts, clinical escalation, downtime readiness, payer/referral/authorization dependencies, repeat failures, low-confidence routing, policy conflict, and normal closure evidence.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build and approval event.
